─[ Safe Edit with Special Characters ]─ ┐

name: safe-edit-special-chars description: Avoid Edit tool failures when matching strings with Unicode or special characters user-invocable: false allowed-tools: Read, Edit

Safe Edit with Special Characters

Context

When using tools to replace strings that contain special or Unicode characters (arrows →, em dashes —, smart quotes, etc.), matching can fail if characters don’t match byte-for-byte.

Snags

  • Invisible Differences: Characters like \u2014 and literal — look identical but may be encoded differently in the file or by the tool.
  • Normalizing Offender: Subagents or summaries often normalize Unicode characters, making copy-pasted strings from their output fail in the Edit tool.
  • Silent Fail: The Edit tool returns “String to replace not found” even when the text is visually present.

Workaround

  1. Source of Truth: Always Read the file immediately before editing strings with special characters.
  2. Copy Direct: Copy the old_string directly from the Read output of the current agent turn.
  3. Avoid Normalization: Do not use strings from earlier session logs or subagent reports for the old_string if they contain Unicode.

Gotchas

  • Arrow characters (→ U+2192) are the most common offenders.
  • Byte-matching is exact; visually identical characters with different encodings WILL fail.
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
